channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Free Sanitary Pad Dispensers Support Girls’ Education Across South Africa
item.description: Over 200 schools in South Africa have introduced free sanitary pad dispensers through the MENstruation Foundation initiative, aiming to combat period poverty and keep girls in school. At Hoërskool Die...

item.title: Kenya Judiciary to Publish Judge Performance Data to Boost Accountability and Transparency
item.description: The Judicial Service Commission (JSC) in Kenya has announced plans to start publishing performance data for individual judges and judicial officers, aiming to strengthen accountability, transparency, ...

item.title: Thames Valley Police Launch Misconduct Investigation into Prince Andrew’s Conduct
item.description: Thames Valley Police have publicly confirmed an investigation into Prince Andrew, Duke of York, over potential misconduct, including corruption and sexual offences. The announcement comes after years ...

item.title: Mother jailed for causing severe injuries to swimmer while drunk on Australia Day at Mannum
item.description: Amy Lohf, a 44-year-old mother of two from Mannum, South Australia, has been sentenced to two years, eight months and 13 days in jail after seriously injuring a young female swimmer while operating a ...

item.title: Singularity South Africa Summit to explore AI, quantum computing and digital innovation in October 2026
item.description: The Singularity South Africa Summit is set to return to the Sandton Convention Centre in Johannesburg on 21 and 22 October 2026, marking its eighth edition in the country. Supported by headline sponso...

item.title: Eastern Cape records highest murder rate in South Africa despite overall national decline
item.description: Despite a national decline in violent crimes, the Eastern Cape continues to have the highest murder rate in South Africa, according to police minister Firoz Cachalia. For the first quarter of the 2025...

item.title: US Sanctions Tanzanian Police Official Over Alleged Torture of Foreign Activists
item.description: The United States has slapped sanctions on a senior Tanzanian police officer over alleged human rights abuses involving Kenyan activist Boniface Mwangi and Ugandan journalist Agather Atuhaire. The duo...

item.title: Jacky Vike Shares How She Learned Kikuyu Through Friendship and Church Involvement
item.description: Former Papa Shirandula actress Jacky Vike, popularly known as Awinja, recently shared the story of how she became fluent in Kikuyu despite coming from a Luhya background. Jacky explained that her inte...

item.title: Presidency Denies Reports About Renaming Nigeria or Abolishing Sharia Law
item.description: The Nigerian Presidency has strongly denied reports claiming that President Bola Tinubu intends to amend the constitution to rename Nigeria as the 'United States of Nigeria' and abolish Sharia law in ...

item.title: Journalist Leaves the U.S. Citing Safety Concerns Amid Epstein Investigations
item.description: Journalist Alisa Valdes-Rodriguez, known for her investigative reporting on Jeffrey Epstein, announced she is leaving the United States due to safety concerns. She claims her home was targeted in atta...

item.title: UK Embassy Denies Claims That PM Starmer Declined Meeting With Gachagua
item.description: The UK Embassy in Nairobi has clarified that a letter circulating online claiming Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer turned down former Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua's meeting request is fake. Gachag...

item.title: Tony Abbott elected unopposed as federal president of the Liberal Party
item.description: Former Prime Minister Tony Abbott has been nominated unopposed as the new federal president of the Liberal Party and will be formally installed at the party's federal council meeting in Melbourne on F...

item.title: Albino buffalo in Bangladesh draws attention for unique golden mane
item.description: A rare albino buffalo in Paikpara, Narayanganj, Bangladesh, has captured public attention due to its unusual golden mane, which some locals say resembles former U.S. President Donald Trump's hairstyle...

item.title: London man sentenced to five years for antisemitic threats
item.description: A 36-year-old man, Tavius Jean-Charles, has been sentenced to five years in prison in London after admitting to multiple antisemitic hate crimes. Between October 2025 and March 2026, Jean-Charles made...

item.title: Punjab government implements measures to control stray dogs after Supreme Court directives
item.description: Punjab Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann has announced a state-wide initiative to manage stray dogs in public areas, following a recent Supreme Court ruling that allows euthanasia for rabid, incurably ill,...

item.title: Retired NBI officials express support for Chief Matibag amid calls for suspension
item.description: MANILA, Philippines — Retired agents and former employees of the National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) have voiced their support for current NBI Director Melvin Matibag and his personnel. The NBI Ret...

item.title: Federal Government Boosts Nigeria’s Tourism and Hospitality Standards
item.description: The Federal Government of Nigeria don really dey push to improve the tourism and hospitality industry to make am dey competitive globally. During one big event at Merit House, Abuja, the National Inst...

item.title: Most Migrants Verified as Legally in South Africa After Durban Operation
item.description: In Durban, authorities conducted a verification operation at the Diakonia Centre following reports that over 500 migrants were displaced from their homes by anti-immigration groups. eThekwini Mayor Cy...

item.title: Meta reaches settlement with Kentucky school district over social media addiction claims
item.description: Meta, along with other major social media companies including TikTok, Snap, and YouTube, has reached a settlement with the Breathitt County School District in Kentucky over claims related to social me...

item.title: Fritz Lang’s Vision of 2026 in Metropolis: A Century-Old Sci-Fi Prediction of Urban Life
item.description: In Fritz Lang’s 1927 cinematic masterpiece Metropolis, the year 2026 is depicted as a highly structured, futuristic city where architecture mirrors social hierarchy. Lang envisioned a towering metropo...
